findings.title: Generated Universal Router artifacts are bound to the test router address
findings.suggested_fix: Do not generate checked-in executable Universal Router artifacts with the hard-coded test executor. Either require the artifact generator to take the actual deployed router address and validate executionContext.executor against BASE_FAME_ROUTER_ADDRESS, or deploy the router deterministically to the encoded executor address. Update the fork coverage so it executes generated artifacts against the same address the deployment/validation flow will use.
findings.why_it_matters: The Solidity Universal Router adapters require the embedded payload recipient to equal the executing FameRouter address. The default artifact compiler encodes Universal Router leg payload recipients as 0x000000000000000000000000000000000000f00d, and the fork test masks this by deploying test code to that address. The production deployment script uses a normal new FameRouter deployment, so generated V3/V4 route artifacts will revert with InvalidUniversalRouterPayload when replayed against the real router unless every artifact is regenerated for that exact address.

evidence.detail: buildCompiledRoute passes context.executor into buildLeg, and the V3/V4 encoders place that value in the Universal Router payload recipient field.
executeV3 rejects payloads whose recipient is not the FameRouter address passed by FameRouter._dispatch.
executeV4 rejects payloads whose recipient is not the FameRouter address passed by FameRouter._dispatch.
The generated-artifact fork test deploys FameRouter bytecode directly to GENERATED_ROUTER, address(0xF00D), so it does not prove the checked-in artifacts execute against a normal deployment address.

findings.title: Base deployment script lacks a chain-id gate before broadcasting
findings.suggested_fix: Read BASE_CHAIN_ID in run() and revert before startBroadcast when block.chainid does not match. Add a deployment-validation test that sets a non-Base chain id and proves no broadcasted deployment/configuration path can proceed.
findings.why_it_matters: The deploy script is Base-specific: it reads BASE_* configuration and enables Base venue target addresses from the manifest. Unlike ValidateFameRouterBase, DeployFameRouter does not check block.chainid, so a misconfigured RPC or forge invocation can broadcast a router configured with Base addresses on the wrong network. That violates the Base deployment intent and produces an unusable deployment that the deployment script itself does not fail closed.

evidence.detail: run() reads Base deployment environment variables but does not read or compare BASE_CHAIN_ID.
Broadcast starts before any chain-id validation in the deployment path.
The validation script has the expected chain-id check, showing the deployment flow has the public config value available but does not enforce it before deployment.

residual_risks: Review was limited to static diff inspection because the user requested no project mutations except the review artifact; I did not run forge or bun tests, which would write build/cache outputs.
Universal Router V3/V4 correctness still depends on live Permit2 and Universal Router semantics on the pinned Base block; the mock coverage does not fully model those contracts.

The router intentionally trusts owner-enabled venue targets and user-supplied route payloads within typed adapters; this review did not re-audit economic route quality or malicious-but-allowed venue behavior.
testing_gaps: Add a test that deploys FameRouter through DeployFameRouter or a normal CREATE path, loads generated solver artifacts, and proves Universal Router routes either execute after executor-specific generation or fail before the fix.
Add a deployment-script negative test for a non-BASE_CHAIN_ID chain before startBroadcast.
Run the Base pinned-fork generated route suite against live RPC after fixing the artifact executor binding.
